EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense in Las Marias indicated that Rio Mayaguecillo went out of its banks flooding road 124.  A mudslide occurred at road 124.
A Roosevelt Roads Navy Base personnel reported a waterspout that made a brief landfall and dissipated shortly near the airport base.
Several creeks went out of their banks due to heavy rains.
Two streams went out of their banks near El Cafetal and La Perla sectors.
A stream overflowed its banks.
Civil Defense indicated streams and creeks out of their banks in Caguas. A car was trapped over a rising creek.
A small stream in Cayey went out of its banks flooding a nearby road. In Naguabo road 31 was reported flooded due to heavy rains.
A small stream in Cayey went out of its banks flooding a nearby road. In Naguabo road 31 was reported flooded due to heavy rains.
Urban flooding was reported over the San Juan Metropolitan area.
Rio Grande de Manati overflowed its banks at road 2 in Manati.

An strong low pressure system developed across the central atlantic producing large north swells across the north coasts of Puerto Rico and the U.S, Virgin Islands.  An associated cold front  moved across the region during the afternoon hours on February 1.  Ahead of the front, winds were light from the north and the air mass was considerably dry. Unusual cooler temperatures were felt during the morning of February 1 across the southern slopes and south coast of Puerto Rico.  Adjuntas a town located in the southern interior  Puerto Rico reported 48 degrees and Ponce in the south coast 56 degrees.   On the morning of February 2, large north swells started to arrive along the north coast of Puerto Rico.  Swells were estimated between 15 to 17 feet over exposed areas.  Several coastal roads in Arecibo, Loiza, Hatillo, Isabela and Aguada were closed due to water, sand and debris on the road. In Arecibo few cabins in a recreational park  were partially damage. In Loiza a few houses also were affected by the seas, and in Aguadilla several fishing boats suffered some damages. .
Heavy rains during the afternoon hours across the northwest Puerto Rico caused that Rio Culebrinas  to overflow its banks over Espinosa sector in Aguada affecting agricultural sectors.
Civil Defense indicated two houses flooded in Trujillo Alto and San Juan.
Civil Defense indicated two houses flooded in Trujillo Alto and San Juan.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several roads flooded in Nauabo due to heacy rains.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense indicated that heavy rains prompted a small stream to overflow its banks flooding various roads between Cabo Rojo and San German.  A car was swept away by the waters. The occupant was  rescued without injuries.
Civil Defense in Cabo Rojo indicated that various homes had roof damages due to the wind and a tree fell on a house. Hail was also observed.

A tourist in Luquillo beach drowned as a consequence of strong rip currents.

A  T.V. Meteorologist indicated that a small stream in San Juan went out of its banks. Several streets were   flooded in Caparra, San Patricio, Buchanan and Puerto Nuevo sectors. Few cars stalled on Kennedy Avenue.
Civil Defense indicated flooding in Cayey and Caguas along La Plata river.
Civil Defense indicated various roads flooded in Caguas.
Strong thunderstorms produced very heavy rainfall and pea size hail in Ponce. In addition urban flooding was reported.
Heavy rain during the morning produced landslides over parts of Fajardo affecting some residences.
A Weather Broadcaster in his off duty hours indicated street  flooding in El Senorial sector in San Juan.
Two men drowned on the Cueva del Indio Beach in Arecibo while fishing. They were swept out to sea by a strong tide surge.
Local Civil Defence indicated that a small stream near near Juncal sector between San Sebastian and Lares flooded three residences damaging the furniture.
Federal Flight  Service indicated a waterspout  just north of  Condado area in San Juan.
Heavy Rain across the Sierra de Luquillo Mountains produced a water rush over Espiritu Santo river in Rio Grande. Two people drowned when they were swept by the waters.

Hurricane Jose reached the northern Leeward Islands Wednesday night, October 20.  As Jose continued to move to the northwest it weakened and by 1100 am Thursday morning Jose was downgraded to Tropical Storm. On its closest point as a Hurricane, Jose passed just to the east northeast of St. Thomas at 800 am Thursday morning, or 40 miles east of Culebra,  65 miles east of Fajardo and 95 miles east of San Juan.  As a Tropical Storm, Jose passed about  55 miles north northeast of San Juan by 200 pm Thursday afternoon. Tropical storm force winds and numerous rain bands affected the eastern sections of Puerto Rico including the islands of Culebra and Vieques during most of Thursday.  In Luquillo, Emergency Management Agency indicated sustained winds from 40 to 45 mph with gusts to 55 mph at around 655 am on October 21. In San Juan maximum sustained winds were 23 mph at 600 am Thursday morning with wind gust of 30 mph and in Ceiba maximum sustained winds were at 28 mph with winds gusts of 37 mph at 800 am. These winds knock down power lines, trees and bent street signs in Culebra and Fajardo.  Heavy rainfall between three to four inches across eastern Puerto Rico caused that Blanco River in Naguabo to overflow its banks. Landslides were reported in Utuado, Carolina and Villalba.

In Loiza a few houses also were affected by the seas, and in Aguadilla several fishing boats suffered some damages. .

Very heavy rain occurred over the northern slopes of Puerto Rico during the afternoon. Doppler Radar indicated around three inches or rain over parts of the northwest Puerto Rico. This rainfall produced a rush of water of an underground river in Lares trapping eighteen people in a cave. One person drowned during the rush of water. They were rescued the next morning.
Street flooding was reported in Buchanan sector in Guaynabo.

Strong thunderstorms developed during the afternoon hours across the central sections of Puerto Rico. Various streams went out of their banks in Utuado, Toa Baja, Barranquitas, Vega Alta and Corozal flooding nearby roads and streets and making some of these roads impassable. Hail was reported in Aibonito and Lares and in Utuado a road was blocked by landslides and another road by fallen trees. Also, in Utuado a car with its occupants was swept away in a small stream. They were rescued later unharmed.

A strong tropical wave produced flash floods throughout the east portion of Puerto Rico. Many streets, highways and roads were flooded. In Naguabo torrential rains caused Blanco River to overflow its banks  flooding road 31. In addition a man was injured due to landslides and schools were closed. In Humacao a house was struck by lightning and in Luquillo an elderly couple was rescued from their home when it flooded as a result of the rains.
A strong rush of water caused a bridge to collapse. Road 123 was closed when heavy rains and winds knocked down a tree, which took down power lines in the area.

A tropical wave caused heavy rains during the morning hours across most of the east and south sections of Puerto Rico. These rains produced flooding of  several highways and landslides. Rio Blanco in Naguago went out of its banks.  A temporarily bridge in Guayama was swept away by rising flood waters.

A tropical wave caused heavy rains during the morning hours across most of the east and south sections of Puerto Rico. These rains produced flooding of  several highways and landslides. Rio Blanco in Naguago went out of its banks.  A temporarily bridge in Guayama was swept away by rising flood waters.

Strong thunderstorms developed during the afternoon hours across the central sections of Puerto Rico. Various streams went out of their banks in Utuado, Toa Baja, Barranquitas, Vega Alta and Corozal flooding nearby roads and streets and making some of these roads impassable. Hail was reported in Aibonito and Lares and in Utuado a road was blocked by landslides and another road by fallen trees. Also, in Utuado a car with its occupants was swept away in a small stream. They were rescued later unharmed.
Civil Defense indicated that Culebrinas river went out of  its banks flooding Palmar sector in Aguadilla, Tablonar sector in Aguada and road 115.

A tropical wave caused heavy rains during the morning hours across most of the east and south sections of Puerto Rico. These rains produced flooding of  several highways and landslides. Rio Blanco in Naguago went out of its banks.  A temporarily bridge in Guayama was swept away by rising flood waters.
Civil Defense reported river flooding along road 2 in Vega Alta.
Civil Defense indicated urban and small stream flooding over parts of Vega Alta.
Civil Defense indicated that a tornado was sighted seven miles southwest of Cresta Blanca sector near road 301.
Heavy rainfall over the northwest Puerto Rico produced a rush of water in a underground river in Lares trapping six people in a cave. They were rescued a few hours later.

Stationary thunderstorms occurred across the San Juan metropolitan area during the afternoon producing widespread flash flooding.  Amount up to 6.00 inches of rain were observed over parts of Rio Piedras sector.  Two major rivers in the San Juan metropolitan area went out of their banks.  The hardest hit areas were the University Gardens, Reparto Metropolitano, and Puerto Nuevo sectors in San Juan where around 100 houses were affected by mud and water. The water level inside the houses was at least two feet, damaging most of their furniture and appliances.  Sidewalks and cars were also covered by mud.  In El Alamo sector in Guaynabo, about 50 families were affected by flooding.
Civil Defense reported river flooding along road 676 in Vega Alta.

Civil Defense indicated that a temporarily bridge collapsed in Barranquas sector in Guayama due  to heavy rains. In addition, several streets were reported flooded in downtown Guayama and the Nigua river in Arroyo went out of its banks near Pitaya sector.
A landslide was reported in Villalba.
Civil Defense notified street flooding in Guayanilla and Penuelas.
Civil Defense reported several houses flooded in Penuelas.  A small stream was reported out of its banks and a cemetery was affected by landslides.
A house in San Sebastian was partially flooded due to poor drainage.

In Humacao a house was struck by lightning and in Luquillo an elderly couple was rescued from their home when it flooded as a result of the rains.
Lighting struck a 16-year old while he was at the beach causing severe burns in his abdominal area.  Another person nearby suffered mild burns.
Civil Defence indicated that a river in El Verde sector in Rio Grande went out of its banks due to heavy rainfall   flooding adjacent areas.
A Weather Service Employee sighted a waterspout near Palomino island just east of Fajardo.
Civil Defense indicated that river Culebrinas went out of its banks flooding Palmar sector in Aguadilla, Tablonar sector in Aguada and road 115.
A funnel cloud was sighted in Carolina and Guaynabo.
Heavy rains produced numerous street flooding and landslides in Vega Alta, Naranjito, Toa Baja and Dorado.
State Civil Defence indicated street flooding due to heavy rainfall in parts of Isabela and Aguadilla.
Civil Defense indicated several creeks and streams out of their banks flooding several sectors in the Bayamon area.
Heavy rains during most of the afternoon across the central sections of Puerto Rico caused the Cibuco River to overflow its banks in  the low lands of Vega Baja.
Weather service personnel indicated that La Plata river near Dorado and the Grande de Loiza river in Loiza and Carolina were causing  significant flooding.
Heavy rains caused a small stream to overflow its banks flooding various streets in La Playa sector.
Heavy rains caused the Rio Orocovis and a small stream in Orocovis to overflow their banks.
Two roads flooded in Hormigueros due to heavy rains.

Hurricane Lenny evolved from a tropical depression over the western Caribbean on Saturday November 13, 1999.  The storm strengthened to a hurricane the evening of November 14 while moving on an unusual track to the east.  At 500 pm on Monday November 15, a hurricane watch was issued for all of Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. The forecast track during that time showed  Hurricane Lenny moving across Puerto Rico Wednesday afternoon.  At 1100 pm, the watch was upgraded to a warning.  By Tuesday afternoon, Lenny was located about 240 miles southwest of Puerto Rico and the first rainbands began to affect the islands.  During Tuesday night and most of Wednesday, Lenny continued to move east-northeast south of Puerto Rico.  Its closest point from Puerto Rico was about 75 miles south-southeast of Maunabo and Vieques on Wednesday morning.  By Wednesday afternoon, Lenny was just 20 miles south of St. Croix. Later that evening it became nearly stationary at about 50 miles east of St. Croix until Thursday afternoon where it began to weaken and move to the northeast away from the local region.  By that time, all the watches and warnings for Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands had been discontinued. The south coast of Puerto Rico suffered some beach erosion, but its major impact across Puerto Rico was the flooding associated with the rainbands.  Some sectors in the east received more than seven inches of rain in a 48 hour period.  This caused many roads to become impassable and many rivers to overflow their banks, flooding low lying areas and forcing communities to be evacuated.  In addition, landslides, rock slides and mud slides were reported along the interior and west sections.  Maximum sustained wind speed at the San Juan airport was 33 mph with gusts up to 39 mph.  In Ceiba, the maximum sustained wind speed was 33 mph with gusts to 48 mph.  These winds knocked down several power lines leaving 22,000 residents without electricity.  Hurricane Lenny's heavy rains caused about 200 farmers in the southeast to lose more than 13 million dollars in production.  More than 80% of the vegetables and 50% of the plantains in the region were lost due to rainfall Tuesday and Wednesday.

Some sectors in the east received more than seven inches of rain in a 48 hour period.  This caused many roads to become impassable and many rivers to overflow their banks, flooding low lying areas and forcing communities to be evacuated.  In addition, landslides, rock slides and mud slides were reported along the interior and west sections.  Maximum sustained wind speed at the San Juan airport was 33 mph with gusts up to 39 mph.  In Ceiba, the maximum sustained wind speed was 33 mph with gusts to 48 mph.  These winds knocked down several power lines leaving 22,000 residents without electricity.  Hurricane Lenny's heavy rains caused about 200 farmers in the southeast to lose more than 13 million dollars in production.  More than 80% of the vegetables and 50% of the plantains in the region were lost due to rainfall Tuesday and Wednesday.

Heavy rains occurred in the afternoon causing flooding in various roads in Gurabo and landslides in Yabucoa where two families were isolated.  In Guayama, a temporarily bridge collapsed, isolating about 300 families in Jagueyes sector.

Civil Defense reported Blanco and Fajardo rivers out of their banks, flooding nearby roads and streets.  In Naguabo, a person was swept away and rescued later unharmed. In Ceiba, a road collapsed due to the rains.

A meteorologist(non nws) indicated urban flooding in Guayama.  Elsewhere, an observer in Juana Diaz reported 4.00 inches of rain during the morning hours.  Various roads were reported flooded in Ponce and Juana Diaz.

A meteorologist(non nws) indicated urban flooding in Guayama.  Elsewhere, an observer in Juana Diaz reported 4.00 inches of rain during the morning hours.  Various roads were reported flooded in Ponce and Juana Diaz.
A cooperative observer indicated flooding along Fajardo River with the main road between Fajardo and Ceiba impassable.
Civil Defense reported a river overflowing a bridge on road 171 near the Rincon sector.
A spotter reported 3.80 inches of rain during the afternoon.  A stream went out of its banks flooding some residences.
Civil Defense reported a landslide in the Quebrada sector of Fajardo. A spotter reported rainfall accumulation of 2.00 to 2.50 inches per hour.
Civil Defense in Manati reported various roads flooded due to heavy rain.
A skywarn spotter reported some streets flooded and impassable in downtown Mayaguez..
NWS employee reported street flooding due to heavy rains.
An employee from Roosevelt Roads Navy Base sighted a waterspout 8 miles southwest of the base in Ceiba.

Heavy rains caused flash flooding and landslides over parts of San Sebastian. Rio Culebrinas went out of its banks flooding a total of 19 houses over Pepino, Pueblo Nuevo and Union Street sectors. In addition fallen trees and two house with their roofs detached were reported due to strong winds.
Civil Defense indicated very heavy rain and strong gusty winds. A house flooded in Barina sector when  a creek overflowed its banks.
A skywarn spotter encountered flooding on highway 114.  He estimated about 2 feet of water on the road.
Civil Defense indicated various mudslides in San Lorenzo, Cayey and Naranjito. Flooding was also reported in various sectors in Cayey.
A small stream and some roads flooded in Puerto Nuevo sector.

Early in the morning scattered showers and thunderstorms moved westward across the eastern sections of Puerto Rico.  This activity was associated with a low level wind surge in combination with an upper jet north of the region.  The most affected municipalities were; Patillas, Guayama, Naguabo, Yabucoa and Maunabo.  Several rivers and streams overflowed their banks flooding low lying areas and roads.

Heavy rains caused two houses to collapse in Bairoa sector in Caguas. Road 796 right across from the houses, broke into pieces. Elsewhere, mudslides affected 15 homes in the Alto Monte sector in Caguas. The mud engulfed their properties and destroyed most of their belongings.  In addition Civil Defense indicated few houses affected by the rain in Morovis.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense indicated few houses affected by the rains in Las Piedras and Juncos.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense in Juana Diaz reported that a flooded stream left highway impassable at Collores sector.

In addition Civil Defense indicated few houses affected by the rain in Morovis.

Heavy rains induced small streams to overflow their banks flooding nearby roads.  At Corrales sector a center for the elderly had to be evacuated, and a few cars were swept away by the waters. Elsewhere, a church was reported flooded.

Civil Defense reported flooding at Bairoa and Las Carolinas neighbourhoods in Caguas. This heavy rainfall caused a house at Bairoa sector to collapse and to slide down a ravine after the road caved in. Elsewhere, a car  was swept away while crossing a flooding sector in Caguas but eventually rescued by Civil Defense personnel.

A big landslide occurred on road 155 in Orocovis. Some cars were trapped.
Civil Defense reported a landslide and flooding on road 354 in Mayaguez.
Civil Defense indicated flooding on road 2, Espinosa sector, in Dorado.
Civil Defense in Vega Alta reported Cibuco River out of its banks, flooding road 620.
Civil Defense reported street flooding in Guaynabo, Catano and San Juan.
Heavy rains flooded various roads in Cayey.
Civil Defense indicated various houses flooded in Rio Grande and Canovanas due to heavy rains.

Heavy rains flooded several roads and highways in Humacao, Naguabo and Las Piedras. Local police also reported that the Guamani river in Guayama overflowed its banks dragging a car and its occupants into the water. They were rescued later unharmed.

Civil Defense indicated flooding along Cibuco river in Vega Alta and numerous landslides in Naranjito.  Fajardo River overflowed its banks flooding roads 3 in Fajardo and 30 in Naguabo.
Civil Defense indicated that three girls, from 14 to 16 years old,  were swept over Rio Grande de Anasco by a water surge.  Two of them were rescued  and one drowned.
Heavy rains produced landslides over Trujillo Alto road 881. A house was affected. In addition road 876 was impassable due to flooding.
A woman in Coco Beach, Rio Grande, sighted a waterspout just north from the beach.

Heavy rain during the afternoon hours over parts of northwest Puerto Rico, upstream of Rio Grande de Anasco, produce a sudden rise in the stage near Anasco. Two fishermen at the mouth of the river  were swept away. One could reach the shore and the other one drowned. The body was recovered about a month later  when the waters receded.
